question.content: In the shown geometry with points A, B, C, D, P, X, Y and the green arcs, determine the target angle in principal-value form. Answer format requirement: <expr>. Strictly forbidden: any extra text beyond this format.

step_1.content: Start from a simple overview of the circle and its main intersecting chords.
step_1.key_point: First lock the base positions of A, B, C, D and the intersection point P.

step_3.content: Add directional arrows and supporting rays to emphasize how the chord relations pass through P.
step_3.key_point: Focus on AP, PC, AC, and BD with intersection-driven relations.

step_5.content: Use auxiliary dashed lines and local angle guidance around P to isolate the target relation.
step_5.key_point: Angle focus is the local configuration around P, X, and the PC direction.

step_7.content: Apply the constrained angle relation and keep only single-expression output.
